use_cases: create one validated ephemeral Standard battle from production identities and an explicit seed
observe a bounded player-visible decision boundary without hidden authored state
select one opaque offered action and settle through enemy and automatic decisions
retry a state-changing request without committing or consuming RNG twice
page retained events and export a complete canonical replay
close or expire an isolated session
run the same control contract in-process, over local stdio MCP, or over authorized Streamable HTTP MCP

decision_owner_matrix.settlement: session applies the exact offered lifecycle command
return opaque offered actions to the caller
session uses authored enemy data and an exact offered command

narrow_application_seams: enumerate and resolve production Standard scenario identities without depending on private CLI code
construct the frozen production battle/activity input from validated scenario and build identities
coordinate authored enemy and automatic decisions while recording controller diagnostics
forbidden_core_changes: no MCP, JSON-RPC, HTTP, authentication, session, operational clock, or idempotency concepts in starclock-combat, starclock-rules, or starclock-activity
no mutation path other than Battle::apply and Activity::apply
no bypass of exact offered Command values or DecisionPoint ownership
no protocol-derived values in canonical state hashes, replay facts, RNG seeds, or deterministic ordering
no public untrusted construction of Rule IR, BattleSpec, or arbitrary catalog definitions
no challenge-mode, universe-mode, durable persistence, model-provider, prompt, or private reasoning surface
